text: My trip to Japan last year was my first solo adventure, and I had spent months planning every detail. From booking flights to researching local customs, I wanted everything to go smoothly. To my surprise, the best parts were the unplanned moments: tasting street food in Osaka, getting lost in Kyoto’s tiny alleys, and struggling to order ramen using only hand gestures. Language barriers were frustrating at times, but they also sparked funny and heartwarming exchanges. Immersing myself in a new culture completely changed my view of travel. Now, the memories of that journey—the flavors, sounds, and friendly strangers—still make me smile.
